title: High-contrast cloth book
description: A soft book of bold black-and-white patterns and simple shapes.
benefit: Engages newborn vision and introduces early book sharing.

title: Baby-safe mirror
description: An unbreakable, freestanding mirror for floor and tummy time.
benefit: Encourages head control and early self-discovery.

title: Jhunjhuna (soft rattle)
description: A lightweight, easy-to-hold rattle or jhunjhuna that makes a gentle sound.
benefit: Builds grasp and links sound with cause and effect.

title: Activity play gym
description: A padded mat with dangling toys to bat and reach for.
benefit: Encourages reaching, batting, and tummy time.

title: Crinkle sensory toy
description: A soft fabric toy with crinkle sounds and varied textures.
benefit: Rewards grasping with sound and offers rich sensory exploration.

title: Easy-grip teether
description: A safe, chewable teether shaped for little hands.
benefit: Soothes gums and develops grasp and hand-to-mouth control.

title: Soft sensory ball
description: A squishy, textured ball that's easy to grab and pass.
benefit: Builds two-handed grasp and hand-to-hand transfer.

title: Stacking cups or katoris
description: Nesting cups, steel katoris, or a ring stacker in graduated sizes.
benefit: Teaches size, order, and fine-motor control through stack-and-topple play.

title: Cloth or board books
description: Soft cloth or thick-page board books with simple pictures — bilingual ones are great.
benefit: Grows vocabulary, attention, and a love of reading.

title: Soft stacking blocks
description: Lightweight fabric or foam blocks that are safe to mouth and throw.
benefit: Supports grasping, stacking, and early cause-and-effect.

title: Cause-and-effect busy toy
description: A toy with buttons, flaps, or pop-ups that respond to a push.
benefit: Rewards exploration and teaches that actions make things happen.

title: Nesting and container toy
description: Cups or boxes to fill, empty, and fit inside one another.
benefit: Builds spatial reasoning and in-and-out fine-motor play.

title: Push or roll toy
description: A sturdy push toy or rolling ball to chase and move.
benefit: Encourages cruising, crawling, and early walking.

title: Simple musical shakers
description: Baby-safe shakers, manjira (bells), or a small dholak to make sounds with.
benefit: Connects action with sound and supports rhythm and listening.

title: Safe kitchen items
description: Steel cups, katoris, a small lid, and a wooden spoon — the best first toys are already in your kitchen.
benefit: Free, endlessly interesting, and great for banging, filling, emptying, and stacking. Check for sharp edges and small parts.
